codeReview.summary: The feature added the requested regressions and partially fixed explicit-id foreign auto-persist/reuse, but the fresh-DB requirement is not actually proven because the new test does not start from a fresh embedded database state.

issues.description: resh_db_save_requires_no_manual_define_table calls nsure_db() instead of resetting to a fresh database. nsure_db() only reuses the shared test runtime, so this test can pass even if prior tests already created the table. The feature requirement explicitly called for proving first save on a fresh embedded DB succeeds without manual DEFINE TABLE, and this test does not establish that precondition.

sharedStateObservations.observation: The worker did not follow the mission rule to make new regressions fail first for this feature, but the rust-library-worker skill only says to run targeted tests and confirm they fail before implementing. It does not explicitly warn reviewers/workers that a fresh DB regression must actively reset or reinitialize the embedded database rather than reusing ensure_db().
sharedStateObservations.evidence: Mission AGENTS.md requires Workers must add failing tests first and specifically lists fresh DB save coverage. The final committed test at core/tests/integration_db.rs:994-1013 only calls ensure_db(), while the worker transcript skeleton shows no pre-fix failing run of this exact test and earlier exploration referenced reinit_db(...) elsewhere instead.

summary: Review failed: explicit-id foreign persistence behavior appears covered, but the fresh-database acceptance criterion is not actually validated because the committed regression reuses an existing DB/runtime instead of proving first-save behavior on a clean embedded database.
